It looks like your server returned an **HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed** error (with an empty body, which caused the JSON parsing error in the UI). 

This happens for two reasons on Next.js/Nginx VPS setups:
1. **CORS Preflight Blocking:** The custom headers we used (`x-app-name`) trigger an HTTP `OPTIONS` preflight request, which your Next.js API route didn't have a handler for. It returned 405.
2. **PM2 User Mismatch:** The previous fix was run as `root`, so `pm2 restart all` didn't actually restart the PM2 process belonging to your regular user. The server was still running older code!

Here is the ultimate fix. We will bypass the CORS headers completely, explicitly add an `OPTIONS` handler, and rebuild.

Run this code on your VPS as `root`:

```bash
cat << 'EOF_BASH' > /root/fix_405.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -e

cd /var/www/masterchief

echo "1. Applying API fixes to bypass CORS..."
cat << 'ROUTE_EOF' > app/api/deploy/route.ts.b64

ROUTE_EOF

base64 -d app/api/deploy/route.ts.b64 | gzip -d > app/api/deploy/route.ts
rm app/api/deploy/route.ts.b64

cat << 'PAGE_EOF' > app/builder/page.tsx.b64

PAGE_EOF

base64 -d app/builder/page.tsx.b64 | gzip -d > app/builder/page.tsx
rm app/builder/page.tsx.b64

echo "2. Rebuilding App..."
npm run build

echo "3. Restarting Server locally across ALL possible PM2 profiles..."
# Stop Next.js if it's running via systemctl
systemctl restart masterchief || true
# Try restarting PM2 for root
pm2 restart all || true
# Try restarting PM2 for ubuntu / masterchief users (if applicable)
su - ubuntu -c "pm2 restart all" 2>/dev/null || true
su - masterchief -c "pm2 restart all" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "✅ Fix applied! Please hard-refresh your browser (Ctrl+Shift+R or Cmd+Shift+R) and try uploading the ZIP again."
EOF_BASH

bash /root/fix_405.sh
```

**Give that a quick try and let me know if the deployment succeeds!**
